extracted_evidence_snippets.title: Neutral hydrogen and optical observations of edge-on galaxies: Hunting for warps
extracted_evidence_snippets.snippet: warp. As a measure of the optical extent of the galaxy we used Ropt (see Sect. 3), which is more representative than R25 , particularly in edge-on galaxies. We found that all the galaxies in our sample that have HI disks more extended than the optical are warped. If we consider each side of the galaxy independently, there is only one exception to this rule: one side of UGC 7125 is warped, while the other remains flat, and both sides have HI more extended than the optical. One of the most striking characteristics of the warps observed here is their asymmetry. To begin with, a considerable percentage of the warped galaxies show a warp only on one 778 I. extracted_evidence_snippets.interpretation: UGC07125 has one warped side while the other remains flat; supports disturbed/asymmetric geometry quarantine.
